---
title: "Working Notes"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Working Notes}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---


| File                          |  Rows | What's in it                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|-------------------------------|------:|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `concepts_brfss.csv`          | 1,734 | One row per concept (= one per distinct variable name). `concept_id` = variable name (case preserved). `domain` populated for 819 concepts from 2012–2015 module labels, blank for the other 915. `concept_label` = earliest year's question text. `subdomain`, `construct_notes`, `canonical_response_type` all blank for you to fill in. |
| `concept_values_brfss.csv`    | 8,265 | Canonical code→label for each concept, taken from the latest year that has non-missing codes. Missing codes (is_missing=TRUE) are excluded.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `concept_map_brfss.csv`       | 5,500 | One row per (survey, year, raw_var_name). `recode_type = "identity"` throughout — no inferred recodes. `question_text_drift` classified by Jaccard similarity against the earliest year: 4,504 identical / 275 minor / 405 moderate / 316 major.                                                                                           |
| `rename_candidates_brfss.csv` |   466 | Review file — pairs of variable names with matching value signatures, matching question text, and non-overlapping years. 44 HIGH, 179 MEDIUM, 243 LOW confidence. This is NOT applied automatically.                                                                                                                                       |


The rename_candidates file is the one worth the most of your attention. The 44 HIGH candidates are almost all genuine BRFSS version-bumps (ADDEPEV2→ADDEPEV3, DIABETES→DIABETE3, PNEU→PNEUVAC3, HIVTEST1→HIVTST6, RFBING5→RFBING6, DRNKANY5→DRNKANY6, SMOKE→SMOKDAY2, etc.). To unify any pair, you edit concepts_brfss.csv to remove the redundant row, and update concept_map_brfss.csv to point both variables' rows at the surviving concept_id. The MEDIUM/LOW tiers need more judgment.

Caveats that may bite you:

concept_id case is preserved from raw data — ADDEPEV3 and addepev3 would be different IDs. BRFSS is mostly uppercase but has some lowercase (ill30d2, ment30d2, rfbing5). Check your concept_id conventions against whatever your package expects.

915 concepts have no domain — those are variables that first appeared in 2016 or later (when BRFSS codebooks stopped shipping module labels). You'll need to assign domains yourself, probably by carrying forward from related 2012–2015 concepts.

All recode_type = "identity" — nothing is harmonized. Any cross-year concept where the coding actually changed (binary→ordinal, reverse-coded, etc.) will need you to edit those rows to inline or function.

R parity not verified this session. My R port of the concept-builder was too slow and got killed. The Python prototype produced these CSVs and passed manual spot-checks, but if you later want to regenerate these from your own R pipeline you'll want to either diff against these or accept the Python outputs as authoritative.
